fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Oregon Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Oregon - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity oregon, how to transfer physical therapist license to oregon, physical therapist compact oregon, oregon physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Oregon Physical Therapist Licensing Board
board.mailing_address:
Oregon Physical Therapist Licensing Board
800 NE Oregon Street, Suite 407
Portland, OR 97232-2187

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Oregon.

steps.title: Register and Apply Online
steps.description: Create an account on the Oregon Licensing Portal (OLP) at olpres.oregon.gov and complete the 'Physical Therapist Endorsement Application,' paying the required fee.

steps.title: Submit License Verifications
steps.description: Arrange for direct verification of your initial and current PT licenses from all states where you have been licensed, sent directly from the respective state boards to the Oregon Physical Therapist Licensing Board.

steps.title: Complete the Oregon Jurisprudence Exam (OR-JE)
steps.description: Upon application submission and fee payment, the Oregon Physical Therapist Licensing Board will provide information to register for and complete the online Oregon Jurisprudence Examination.

steps.title: Undergo Criminal Background Check (CBC)
steps.description: Follow instructions from the Oregon Physical Therapist Licensing Board to submit fingerprints for a criminal background check if you are an Oregon resident or have been in the past five years.

steps.title: Receive License
steps.description: Once all requirements are met and verified, the Oregon Physical Therapist Licensing Board will issue your Oregon Physical Therapist license.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Oregon physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $195. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Oregon have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Oregon participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Oregon without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Oregon?
faqs.answer: Current board processing time is 4 to 6 Weeks once your file is complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Oregon while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Oregon
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in Oregon, you must create an account and submit your application through the Oregon Licensing Portal (OLP).

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Go to the Oregon Licensing Portal (OLP) at olpres.oregon.gov. Click 'Register for an account' to create a new user profile. Once registered, log in and select 'Apply for a New License/Renewal' to start your PT application.
